comments.author: @telehappy_usa
comments.text: Why are there so many beautiful girls on the pedestrian streets in France?

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: "Bonjour! 🇫🇷 ✨ Warm greetings! Are you tuning in from France today?"

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: "Appreciate the love from a fellow travel documentary channel! 🎬 ✈️ What specific visual or storyline stood out to you most in this segment?

comments.author: @WorldUnveiled-z8
comments.text: France is famous for its beauty and culture, so I was very curious about daily life outside of the famous tourist spots. What surprised you most about France while making this video?

replies.author: @WorldUnveiled-z8
replies.text: @reallifeincountries In your opinion, what aspect of the French "slow living" lifestyle is most worth learning from?

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Điều bất ngờ nhất chính là nhịp sống 'slow living' cực kỳ bài bản của họ! 🇫🇷 🥖 Dù là thành phố lớn, họ vẫn dành trọn thời gian cho bữa trưa và tận hưởng không khí cộng đồng. Nếu có cơ hội đến Pháp, bạn muốn trải nghiệm nhịp sống ở Paris hay một ngôi làng cổ miền Nam hơn?"

comments.author: @RealLifeFilesDoc
comments.text: Bonjour! The segment on the modern French working system completely blew my mind. In a world obsessed with burnout, the fact that France legally enforces a 35-hour work week, 5 weeks of vacation, and the "right to disconnect" from emails after work [24:52] is the ultimate flex of "slow living." We really have so much to learn from their dedication to turning ordinary moments into memories rather than just working constantly. 10/10 documentary! ❤

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: High praise from a top creator! 🇫🇷 💼 France's 'right to disconnect' is definitely a game-changer against modern burnout. As a producer, do you think other Western countries will ever adopt a similar work culture?"
